topic.name: Health Care and Support Services
topic.description: The documents collectively address various health care and support services aimed at improving the well-being of specific populations, including veterans, individuals with disabilities, and children from low-income families.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to authorize appropriations to carry out the Act.
Establishes an Indian Catastrophic Health Emergency Fund (CHEF) to meet extraordinary medical costs.
Document 2: Amends the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 to extend supported employment services.
Authorizes grants to help youths with significant disabilities transition to adulthood.
Document 3: Authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to pay burial and funeral expenses for veterans.
Determines scheduling for a veteran's appeal hearing at the earliest possible date.
Document 4: Expands the authority of the Attorney General to make competitive grants under the public safety and community policing grant program.
Authorizes grants for hiring school resource officers and establishing local partnerships.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot matching grants to states for high-quality prekindergarten programs.
Reserves funding for Indian tribes and tribal organizations.
Document 6: Directs the Assistant Secretary for Children and Families to require minimum standards for programs serving children with emotional or behavioral problems.
Establishes a hotline to receive complaints about child abuse in residential programs.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to evaluate mental health care and suicide prevention programs.
Directs the establishment of a pilot program to assist veterans transitioning from active duty.
Document 8: Expresses the sense of Congress that the VA should provide health care for veterans with traumatic brain injury.
Requires individualized plans for rehabilitation and reintegration of veterans with TBI.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to contract for an independent assessment of scheduling appointments for veterans.
Authorizes the Secretary to recruit highly qualified health care providers for staffing shortages.
Document 10: Amends the Violence Against Women Act to expand definitions and grant conditions.
Enhances judicial and law enforcement tools to combat violence against women.

topic.name: Tariff Suspensions and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, specifically related to the temporary suspension of duties on various products and materials.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on handheld electric can openers.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain injection-molded ABS or PP cases or containers.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain 6-volt batteries.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain adjustable metal lighting fixtures.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily provide for the duty-free treatment of Imidacloprid.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on a complex chemical compound.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on a mixture of specific chemical compounds.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily suspend the duty on certain battery operated ice cream makers.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain high-performance loudspeakers.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Ethics
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, ethics, and transparency in government operations and elections.

Document 1: Prohibits a Member of Congress... from receiving an annuity or retired pay if such Member is: (1) convicted of bribery of public officials...
Defines 'Member of Congress' as the Vice President, a member of the Senate or the House of Representatives...
Document 2: This bill amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and the Internal Revenue Code to revise rules for voting on the suspension of pension benefits...
The bill changes the voting procedure for suspending plan benefits...
Document 3: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to establish within the Election Assistance Commission an Office of the Voter Advocate...
Directs the Office to establish a Democracy Index Pilot Program for calendar year 2008...
Document 4: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule...
Directs the agency head to notify the designated official of each state within 15 days after such a brief is filed...
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate... from employing the spouse or any immediate family member...
Amends the federal criminal code to subject to specified criminal and civil penalties any spouse of a Member of Congress...
Document 6: Amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 to provide a point of order against consideration of any budget resolution that sets forth totals for any fiscal year with respect to the Social Security Trust Funds...
Makes this Act inapplicable upon the enactment of such legislation.
Document 7: This bill amends the Ethics in Government Act of 1978 to require candidates for nomination or election to the office of President or Vice President to include in financial disclosure reports...
Each candidate who won the nomination of a political party for election to the office of President or Vice President in 2016 must comply with this bill by September 26, 2016.
Document 8: Requires each federal and state court to recognize any lawful notarization occurring in or affecting interstate commerce...
Requires such a notarization to use a seal of office as symbol of the notary public's authority...
Document 9: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to revise requirements for the audit capacity of voting systems...
Requires each paper ballot used to be suitable for a manual audit...
Document 10: Extends the temporary office of 30 bankruptcy judgeships authorized or extended under the Bankruptcy Judgeship Act...
Increases by a specified amount the bankruptcy filing fee for a case commenced under chapter 11...

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: The documents collectively address the temporary suspension of duties on various products within the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain inflatable swimming pools.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain three-ring binders wholly or predominantly covered with polyester fabrics.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain swimming pools.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain toric shaped polarized materials.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Desmedipham in bulk or mixtures.
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on 2-Oxepanone, polymer with 2-ethyl-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-propanediol.
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on a certain ion exchange resin powder.
Document 8: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on cerium sulfide pigments.
Document 9: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of Reactive Black 5, Benzenesulfonic acid, and 1-Naphthalenesulfonic acid.
Document 10: extend the suspension of duty on certain mixtures of alkene polymers.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing government accountability, protecting individual rights, and ensuring transparency in government operations and political spending.

Document 1: Amends False Claims Act liability provisions
Allows the government to move to dismiss from a civil action for false claims a qui tam relator.
Document 2: Makes ineligible for federal funds any condemning authority or acquiring party that engages in taking or condemnation not for public use
Requires any entity applying for federal funds to certify eligibility.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal entity from compelling a covered person to testify
Defines 'covered person' as a person engaged in journalism.
Document 4: Prohibits independent expenditures and payments for electioneering communications by government contractors
Applies the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to foreign-controlled domestic corporations.
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act to authorize targeting of persons located outside the United States
Requires the AG and DNI to assess compliance with required targeting and minimization procedures.
Document 6: Requires the FBI to include a statement that the information sought is relevant to an authorized investigation
Enumerates minimization procedures applicable to FBI retention and dissemination.
Document 7: Requires each agency head to submit a monthly report for each rule expected to propose or finalize
Requires the Administrator to make such reports publicly available on the Internet.
Document 8: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act to allow a person's judgment against a terrorist party to be satisfied with blocked assets
Protects the interests of non-terrorist third parties in the blocked assets.
Document 9: Requires the FTC to promulgate regulations requiring security policies for personal information
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to prohibit contributions by recipients of TARP assistance
Requires corporations to file a certification with the FEC before making contributions.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to Medicare and Medicaid programs, addressing coverage, cost negotiation, and service provisions.

1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend Medicare coverage to medical nutrition therapy services for people with pre-diabetes and risk factors for developing type-2 diabetes.
2: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers the prices that may be charged to Medicare part D prescription drug plan (PDP) sponsors.
3: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to repeal the exclusion from medical assistance under the Medicaid program of items and services for patients in an institution for mental diseases.
4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the Medicare outpatient rehabilitation therapy caps.
5: Requires that all monies designated for union health insurance plans or funds be, without exception, fully reciprocated to a worker's home health plan or fund.
6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the caps on Medicare outpatient rehabilitation physical therapy services and speech-language pathology services.
7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for payments of direct graduate education costs under title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act.
8: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow chiropractors to provide items and services through private contracts.
9: Reauthorizes through FY2018 a pediatric specialty educational loan repayment program for health professionals providing pediatric medical subspecialty.
10: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require drug manufacturers to pay rebates to state prescription drug discount programs.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative documents focused on improving healthcare access, coverage, and quality for various populations.

Document 1: Increases Medicare payments to qualifying Medicare providers by 10% for cancer screening tests recommended by the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force.
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to waive cost-sharing for colorectal cancer screening tests.
Document 2: Requires a group health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits and also provides benefits for prosthetics and custom orthotics to offer such prosthetics and custom orthotics in the same manner as applicable to medical and surgical benefits.
Prohibits separate financial requirements or more restrictive treatment limitations.
Document 3: Repeals the health insurance and health coverage expansion requirements of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that the laws of the state designated by a health insurance issuer apply to individual health insurance coverage.
Document 4: Establish a Geriatric and Gerontology Loan Repayment Program to enter into contracts with physicians, physician assistants, nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, psychologists, and social workers trained in geriatrics or gerontology.
Authorize the Secretary to award nursing education grants and enter into contracts for programs that focus on specialty training in providing long-term care services.
Document 5: Allows a veteran enrolled in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) annual patient enrollment system to elect to receive covered health services through a non-VA facility.
Directs the Secretary to authorize such services pursuant to a contract with such facility.
Document 6: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate health care entitlements for certain individuals who are under age 65.
Eliminates requirements that Medicaid benchmark benefits provide minimum essential coverage.
Document 7: Prohibits the federal government from discriminating or taking an adverse action against a child welfare service provider that declines to provide services conflicting with their sincerely held beliefs.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to withhold 15% of the federal funds that a state receives for such programs if the state violates this Act.
Document 8: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require a state Medicaid plan to provide 12-month continuous enrollment for an eligible individual.
Directs the Secretary to identify methods that promote the retention of individuals who are enrolled under the state plan.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct research to assess the standardization of critical care protocols.
Amends the Food, Agriculture, Conservation and Trade Act of 1990 to authorize appropriations for augmenting the delivery of critical care services.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require a group or individual health plan that covers anticancer medications administered by a health care provider to provide no less favorable coverage for anticancer medications administered by a patient.
These requirements apply to medication approved by the Food and Drug Administration that is prescribed based on a finding by the treating physician.

topic.name: Transportation and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety and regulatory standards in transportation and food safety sectors.

Document 1: Mandate Oil Spill Investigations and Emergency Rules (MOSIER) Act of 2016
investigate derailments involving a significant release of hazardous materials
Document 2: Surface Transportation Safety Act of 2011
improve traffic signs and pavement markings for older drivers and pedestrians
Document 3: Small Community Options for Regulatory Equity Act of 2007
exempt any nonprofit small public water system from the requirements of any national primary drinking water regulation
Document 4: Processed Food Safety Act of 2009
prohibit the sale or transport in commerce of any processed poultry, poultry product, meat, or meat food product capable of human consumption that has not undergone a pathogen reduction treatment
Document 5: Imported Seafood Safety Standards Act
prohibit the importation of any seafood from a foreign country unless the country complies with U.S. standards
Document 6: Preserve Access to Affordable Generics Act
authorize the Federal Trade Commission to initiate a proceeding against parties to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim
Document 7: General Aviation Security Enhancement Act of 2009
prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from issuing a rule regarding transportation security without a negotiated rulemaking committee
Document 8: Biometric Enhancement for Airport-Risk Reduction Act of 2008
study and report on how airports can transition to uniform biometric identifier systems
Document 9: Ensuring Patient Access and Effective Drug Enforcement Act of 2016
amends the Controlled Substances Act to define phrases related to the DEA's authority
Document 10: Food Freshness Disclosure Act of 2007
deem food to be misbranded unless its label bears information on freshness

topic.name: Environmental Policy and Economic Development
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation aimed at improving environmental quality, enhancing agricultural practices, and promoting economic development through workforce investment and innovation.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize the Chesapeake Bay Program.
Expanding the implementation and monitoring grant programs.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that increasing the competitiveness of U.S. manufacturers will strengthen the national economy.
Investing in America's Workforce Act requires state or local workforce investment systems to use youth activities funds allocated to a local area for programs that provide training.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce to 25% of taxable income the income tax rate for corporations.
States that the purpose of this title is to increase accountability for and transparency in the federal regulatory process.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to offer to enter into a risk management account with a qualifying farm or ranch operator.
Links such payments to environmental stewardship.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to publish in the Federal Register a draft methodology for determining which minerals qualify as critical minerals.
Establishes within the Department of the Interior the Critical Minerals Working Group.
Document 6: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President a national energy security program to coordinate federal activities and policies to reduce oil consumption.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop a national plan for electric drive vehicle deployment.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish the Private-Public Partnership Challenge Grant Program.
Authorizes the Secretary to provide grants to states for use in implementing innovative strategies.
Document 8: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to give the operator, tenant, or sharecropper on a farm an opportunity to make an annual election to receive aggregate risk and revenue management payments.
Extends the environmental quality incentives program.
Document 9: Authorizes appropriations for economic development assistance programs under such Act for FY2011-FY2015.
Encouraging and supporting public-private partnerships that promote innovation and entrepreneurship.
Document 10: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline.
Grants original and exclusive jurisdiction to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit over any civil action for the review of a federal agency action regarding the pipeline.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Refugee Resettlement
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative efforts related to immigration reform, including provisions for waivers of inadmissibility, refugee resettlement, and protections for specific immigrant groups.

1: nothing in this Act shall be construed to enable the Attorney General or the Secretary of Homeland Security to expand his or her discretionary authority
waiving inadmissibility for certain persons who entered the United States before age 16
2: set forth requirements for programs for domestic resettlement of, and assistance to, refugees
the state shall be reimbursed by the federal government for expenses incurred
4: includes immigrants with advanced degrees in the diversity immigrant category
establishes the annual worldwide limit for such immigrants at 18,333
5: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to revise provisions regarding the detention and removal of aliens ordered removed
extends the removal (and detention) period beyond 90 days
10: authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional permanent resident status, an alien who entered the United States prior to his or her sixteenth birthday
is a person of good moral character

topic.name: Federal Security and Efficiency Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing security, efficiency, and operational effectiveness within federal agencies, particularly in transportation and data management.

Document 1: optimize the usage and efficiency of federal data centers
achieve an average power utilization effectiveness for its data centers of 1.2 or less by 2015
Document 2: develop specific prioritized recommendations for improving rail security
establish a task force to complete a risk assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation
Document 3: authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings
Document 4: authorizes FY2013-FY2014 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors
Document 5: direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to establish procedures for designating a small business as an emerging business enterprise
requires the head of each federal agency to establish goals for participation by the designated EBEs
Document 6: authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Coast Guard
acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation
Document 7: authorizes FY2015 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors related to search and rescue
Document 8: authorizes appropriations for FY2010-FY2011 for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation programs related to maritime technology
Document 9: requires each contract awarded by a federal agency that includes private security functions to provide specified information
prohibits any agency from awarding a covered contract for performance of activities that require direct participation by contractor personnel in hostilities
Document 10: complete a vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation
develop specific prioritized recommendations for improving rail security

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on National Security
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing U.S. national security concerns through diplomatic, military, and economic measures.

Document 1: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Limits the use of such resources to such programs only.
Document 2: States that it is U.S. policy to recognize Jerusalem as the undivided capital of Israel.
Limits appropriations that may be obligated in FY2015 for Department acquisition and maintenance of buildings abroad until the U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem has officially opened.
Document 3: Prohibits the President from using Department of Defense (DOD) funds to transfer or release to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other detainee.
No amounts authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available for DOD for FY2016 may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo.
Document 4: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 5: Requires the Director of Foreign Assistance to establish and carry out a program to assist eligible Sudanese refugees in the United States.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Director should consult with the Lost Boys/Lost Girls Organization in the United States to identify interested and eligible program candidates.
Document 6: Directs the Secretaries of Commerce, of Defense, of Energy, of the Interior, and of State to appoint an Executive Agent to serve as a representative on an interagency working group.
Requires the Secretary of Defense to commence procurement of critical rare earth materials and place them in a national stockpile.
Document 7: Directs that, if the President is unable to make a determination that the government of Iraq is achieving certain security, political, and economic benchmarks, the Secretary of Defense shall commence the redeployment of U.S. Armed from the non-Kurdish areas of Iraq.
Prohibits any funds from being obligated or expended to deploy or continue the deployment of U.S. Armed Forces in such areas after the 180-day redeployment period.
Document 8: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should use diplomatic and economic means to resolve the Iranian nuclear problem.
States that nothing in this Act shall be construed as authorizing the use of force or the use of the U.S. Armed Forces against Iran.
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to assess the effectiveness of antiterrorism measures maintained at foreign ports.
Prohibits this Act from requiring the appropriate Secretary to recognize an assessment conducted by a foreign government or international organization.
Document 10: Prohibits the head of any executive agency from entering into a contract with Rosoboronexport regarding the design, manufacture, or sale of military equipment.
Authorizes the President to waive such prohibition if Rosoboronexport has ceased the transfer of military equipment to Syria.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at reforming tax policies for individuals, businesses, and specific groups such as farmers and seniors.

Document 1: This bill amends provisions of the Internal Revenue Code relating to the taxation of small business entities.
TITLE I--SMALL BUSINESS TAX REFORM
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to: (1) allow individual taxpayers a refundable tax credit for health insurance costs paid for the benefit of the taxpayer, the taxpayer's spouse, and dependents.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize an individual or a person engaged in business activity to make an irrevocable election to be subject to a flat tax.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to limit the penalty for failure to disclose a reportable transaction.
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to private activity bond financing for first-time farmers.
Document 7: This bill directs the Internal Revenue Service to make available to taxpayers who have turned age 65 at the close of the taxable year a new federal income tax Form 1040SR.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to treat a bond issued for a specified purpose as a tax credit Build America Bond.
Document 9: This bill amends the FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012 with respect to rollovers to a traditional individual retirement account (IRA).
Document 10: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a taxpayer to accelerate the American Opportunity Tax Credit for qualified tuition and related expenses.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and acts related to federal policies and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Directs U.S. Customs and Border Protection to provide for the liquidation or reliquidation of certain entries of medium density fiberboard.
Document 2: Amends title IV part D of the Social Security Act to provide for the mandatory revocation of passports of individuals who are more than $2,500 in arrears in child support payments.
Document 3: Prohibits the screening of a minor passenger by a pat down search under the supervision of the TSA without parental consent.
Document 4: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to issue and sell a veterans health care stamp to fund medical care for veterans.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require grants for states experiencing a shortage of nursing faculty.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to include a citizenship question in the decennial census.
Document 7: Makes permanent the requirement for the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish headstones for eligible veterans.
Document 8: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to treat Puerto Rico as a state under chapter 9.
Document 9: Repeals provisions of the federal criminal code that prohibit firearm possession in school zones.
Document 10: Amends the Higher Education Act to adjust Perkins Loan borrowing limits for inflation.

topic.name: Community Health and Safety Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing community safety, health services, and support systems through collaborative approaches and targeted programs.

Document 1: establish a grant program for governments, nonprofit organizations, and institutions of higher education to prepare a comprehensive plan and enhance community capacity to address crime
Document 2: authorize grants to implement a mental health first aid training program
recognize the signs and symptoms of mental illness
Document 3: provide job training and placement services for older adults
partner with organizations to provide training for workforce technology skills
Document 4: modify the public safety and community policing grant program
authorize the use of such grants to hire school resource officers
Document 5: fund demonstration projects to promote universal access to family centered, evidence-based interventions
screen caregivers for child maltreatment risk factors
Document 6: develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
provide comprehensive technical assistance to drug courts
Document 7: provide basic health and support services to medically underserved populations
ensure accessibility of services to underserved populations
Document 8: coordinate and implement priority state- and community-led restoration plans and projects
develop cooperative strategies that address the water quality and needs of Gulf resources
Document 9: focus on care coordination and service delivery for older individuals with chronic illness
give direct-care workers opportunities for career advancement
Document 10: establish the Hurricanes Katrina and Rita Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program
provide grants to test, implement, and evaluate models of cultural competence training

topic.name: Renewable Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy production, enhancing energy storage, and regulating fossil fuel emissions.

Document 1: require the Department of Energy to consider the objective of improving the conversion, use, and storage of carbon dioxide
carbon capture research and development
Document 2: increase to 27.5% the percentage depletion allowance for domestic crude oil and natural gas production
require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish cost-effective minimal requirements for the prevention of oil and hazardous substance discharges
Document 3: allow a 20% energy tax credit for investment in energy storage property
make such property eligible for new clean renewable energy bond financing
Document 4: authorize grants to eligible coastal states to support voluntary state efforts to initiate and complete surveys of portions of coastal state waters
identify potential areas suitable or unsuitable for the exploration, development, and production of renewable energy
Document 5: require that the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) contain at least 30 million barrels of refined petroleum products
prohibits drawdown and sale of SPR petroleum products unless required by a severe energy market supply interruption
Document 6: provide funding for projects to improve the movement of goods in interstate commerce
mitigate environmental damage caused by the movement of goods
Document 7: prescribe requirements for a Federal Renewable Portfolio Standard
specifies a schedule of graduated annual percentages of a supplier's base amount that shall be generated from renewable energy resources
Document 8: establish a program to deploy and integrate plug-in electric drive vehicles
demonstrate the viability of a vehicle-based transportation system that is not overly dependent on petroleum
Document 9: revise the renewable fuel program by prohibiting the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from requiring that transportation fuel contain any applicable volume of renewable fuel
waives at least 20% of any of the applicable volume requirement
Document 10: allow federally-guaranteed loans for renewable fuel pipelines
fuel that is produced from renewable biomass and that is used to replace or reduce the quantity of fossil fuel

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer protection in financial services and improving transparency in mortgage and credit markets.

Document 1: Establishes a Federal Board of Certification to certify that the mortgages within a security instrument meet the underlying standards
The purpose of this certification process is to increase the transparency, predictability, and reliability of securitized mortgage products.
Document 2: Prohibit predispute arbitration agreements with consumers
Related to credit card accounts or personal bank accounts.
Document 3: Prohibits, after the initial review of any family's fixed income, the public housing agency from being required to review its income for any year
Requires PHAs to also review a family's income receiving Section 8 at least once every three years.
Document 4: Requires the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to grant to a state or federally recognized Indian tribe a five-year waiver from a final rule
To regulate payday loans, vehicle title loans, or other similar loans.
Document 5: Require federal financial institution regulatory agencies to establish a single, toll-free telephone number for consumer complaints
Concerning institutions under their jurisdiction.
Document 6: Establishes the Commission on the Foreclosure and Mortgage Lending Crisis to study and report to Congress
On the current legal and regulatory framework governing the housing mortgage lending markets.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act to require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report
Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination.
Document 8: Direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to establish a program to provide increased access to federal contract opportunities
For early stage small businesses.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans
Exceeding specified limits.
Document 10: Requires that any mailed unsolicited commercial offer for any financial product or service include a label
This Is an Unsolicited Commercial Offer From.

topic.name: Federal Land Transfers and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to the transfer, acquisition, and management of federal lands, particularly focusing on their use for public benefit, environmental considerations, and specific provisions for Native American tribes.

Document 1: Provides for the sale of specified federal land in Winn Parish, Louisiana
Requires sale proceeds to be used for the acquisition of lands and interests in the Kisatchie National Forest in Louisiana.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to take into trust 4 tracts of federal land in New Mexico
Requires the federal lands taken into trust to be used for the educational, health, cultural, business, and economic development of the 19 pueblos.
Document 3: Bars the Secretary of the Interior from using federal funding to purchase land resulting in a net increase of land acreage
Makes such prohibition inapplicable to easements acquired by the Secretary of the Interior.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, through the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to convey to the state of Utah
Requires the state to pay the United States the fair market value of that part of the conveyed land.
Document 5: Modifies the boundaries of the Pisgah National Forest in McDowell County, North Carolina
Requires an owner's consent to acquire the private land.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to them by sale all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to any portion of approximately 19 acres of certain federal land
Requires deposit of the proceeds of any such conveyance in a special account.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to convey approximately five acres of certain identified National Forest System land
Requires any proceeds received by the Secretary from any cash equalization payment or sale to be deposited in a special account.
Document 8: Requires the United States to hold in trust lands for the benefit of various Native American tribes
Gaming is prohibited on these lands.
Document 9: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to implement administrative and procedural changes
Sets forth deadlines for the processing of transfer applications.
Document 10: Allows the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands anywhere within Arizona and New Mexico to be exchanged for lands
Authorizes the Navajo Tribe to designate up to 150,000 acres within specified lands to be designated as Navajo Sovereignty Empowerment Zones.

topic.name: Education Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving educational outcomes and support systems for students across different educational levels.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans
Document 2: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to include on the same website that is used to provide students with the electronic version of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid
Requires that website to also include detailed guidance on how dependent students who satisfy those immigration requirements may obtain student assistance
Document 3: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Requires states to adopt challenging academic content and achievement standards for mathematics, reading or language arts, and science
Document 4: Revises requirements for statewide and local workforce investment systems to include adult education activities
Amends the AEFLA to include among adult education and literacy services programs activities to assist adults with limited English proficiency
Document 5: Directs the Environmental Protection Agency to establish a voluntary school and child care lead testing program
assists local educational agencies in voluntary testing for lead contamination in drinking water
Document 6: Amends part B of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program
Raises federal grant limits when funds available to implement the program equal or exceed $100 million
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Education to award formula grants to states for allocation to their local educational agencies to reduce class size
Permits LEAs to use such funds to recruit highly qualified teachers
Document 8: Requires each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to IHEs to improve programs to prevent harassment
Document 9: Allows each state to enter into a five-year performance agreement with the Secretary of Education permitting it to receive federal funds
Requires each agreement to be approved by a combination of specified state parties
Document 10: Requires the Secretary of Education to make matching grants of at least $1 million to states for allocation to local educational agencies
Includes school counselors, school psychologists or other psychologists, child or adolescent psychiatrists, and school social workers among such providers

topic.name: Unemployment and Housing Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to unemployment compensation, housing finance reform, and assistance programs aimed at improving access to housing and employment.

Document 1: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014
extends emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals.
Document 4: Housing Finance Reform and Taxpayer Protection Act of 2014
eliminates Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Document 7: Section 8 Voucher Reform Act of 2008
revises requirements for public housing agency inspections.

topic.name: U.S. Postal and Infrastructure Designations
topic.description: Documents related to the designation of postal facilities and infrastructure changes in the United States, particularly focusing on the Great Lakes region.

1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 440 2nd Avenue in Gallipolis, Ohio, as the 'Bob Evans Post Office Building.'
2: Designates the U.S. courthouse and federal building located at 118 South Mill Street, Fergus Falls, Minnesota, as the 'Edward J. Devitt United States Courthouse and Federal Building.'
3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 99 West 2nd Street in Fond du Lac, Wisconsin, as the Lieutenant Colonel James 'Maggie' Megellas Post Office.
5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the harbor maintenance tax commercial cargo (other than bulk cargo) loaded or unloaded at U.S. ports in the Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System.
6: Requires the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service to study and report to Congress on methods for eradicating Asian carp from the Illinois Waterway System.

topic.name: Veteran Health and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care access, mental health services, and benefits for veterans and their families.

Document 1: provides a physical examination to each member of a reserve component
requires the Secretary concerned to provide such examination during the 90-day period before such member's scheduled date of separation
Document 2: makes a veteran who served on active duty during a period of war eligible for a mental health evaluation
requires post-deployment medical and mental health screenings to be conducted within 30 days after a deployment
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Defense to identify the most appropriate and effective means for the preservation by the DOD of documentary evidence of incidents of sexual assault or harassment
requires the Secretary and the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a joint task force
Document 4: includes as a preference eligible for federal employment purposes a parent of an individual who lost his or her life under honorable conditions while serving in the Armed Forces
Document 6: directs the Department of Defense to review the prescribing practices at military treatment facilities of pharmaceutical agents for post-traumatic stress treatment
Document 8: directs the Secretary of Defense to enhance and improve DOD programs to provide family support for families of members of the National Guard and Reserve undergoing deployment
Document 9: eliminates the minimum continuous active duty service requirement for homeless veterans to receive certain benefits
defines veteran for purposes of such benefits as a person who served in the active military, naval, or air service

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Regulation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating specific industries, protecting consumer rights, and addressing public health and safety issues.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the civil penalties for violations of firearms law
Requires fines to be based upon the nature and severity of the violation
Document 2: States that it is U.S. policy that all nonambulatory livestock in interstate and foreign commerce be immediately and humanely euthanized
Amends the Humane Methods of Slaughter Act of 1958 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to promulgate regulations
Document 3: Prohibits the imposition of any penalty under an Act of Congress for the possession of marijuana for personal use
Deems the possession of 100 grams or less of marijuana as personal use
Document 4: Amends the Indian Arts and Crafts Act of 1990 to authorize any federal law enforcement officer to conduct an investigation of an offense
Requires that the findings of an investigation of such an alleged violation by any federal department or agency be submitted to a federal or state prosecuting authority
Document 5: Prohibits a state or local jurisdiction from imposing multiple or discriminatory taxes on the sale or use of a digital good or service
Restricts taxation of a digital good or service to taxation by a state or local jurisdiction whose territorial limits encompass a customer tax address
Document 6: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that the required parental consent or notification took place
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem to be misbranded any drug intended for human use that contains an ingredient that constitutes or is derived from a grain or starch-containing ingredient
Label fails to include a parenthetical statement identifying the source of the ingredient
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to expand mail and wire fraud prohibitions
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to review and amend its guidelines and policy statements
Document 9: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require pharmacies to comply with certain rules related to contraceptives
Provides that a pharmacy is not prohibited from refusing to provide a contraceptive to a customer if it is unlawful to dispense the contraceptive
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit knowingly removing, obliterating, tampering with, or altering a mobile device identification number
Subjects any person who violates such prohibition to a fine, not more than five years' imprisonment, or both.

topic.name: Natural Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and utilization of natural resources, including oil, gas, fisheries, and public lands.

1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to implement any agreement for the management of transboundary hydrocarbon reservoirs

Authorizes the Secretary to implement the terms of the Agreement between the United States of America and the United Mexican States Concerning Transboundary Hydrocarbon Reservoirs in the Gulf of Mexico
4: Directs the Gulf States Marine Fisheries Commission to prepare and adopt a data collection strategy for the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery
Requires each Gulf coastal state to submit for the Commission's approval appropriate management measures that ensure compliance with the objectives of the fishery management plan
3: Authorizes the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide financial assistance to hire additional personnel to assist the state or tribe with expertise in fields relevant to consideration of federal refinery authorizations
Directs the President to appoint a federal coordinator to facilitate such authorizations
10: Directs the Bureau of Land Management to establish priority and variance areas on covered land for geothermal, solar, and wind energy projects
Establishes in the Treasury the Renewable Energy Resource Conservation Fund, to be available in regions affected by the development of wind or solar energy on federal land

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: A collection of legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code addressing various tax-related issues and provisions.

Document 1: repeal the prohibition against churches and other tax-exempt organizations participating in political campaigns
supporting or opposing candidates for public office
Document 2: make permanent the classification of certain farming business machinery and equipment as five-year property
tax deduction for depreciation
Document 3: allow proceeds of qualified mortgage bonds to be used to refinance certain residential subprime loans
increase limitations on issuance amounts for qualified mortgage bonds
Document 4: make permanent the election to deduct state and local sales taxes
in lieu of state and local income taxes
Document 5: eliminate the restriction on nonprofit corporations that acquire student loan notes
expand the operations of such corporations to include performing student assistance related activities
Document 6: permit a state to offset state income tax obligations owed by nonresidents against any federal income tax refunds
if such state has in effect a reciprocal agreement
Document 7: increase to 58.5 cents per mile the standard mileage rate for the tax deduction
for the charitable use of a passenger automobile
Document 8: increase the standard mileage rate for use of a passenger automobile for charitable purposes
exclude from the gross income of charitable volunteers reimbursements paid to them
Document 9: redefine 'applicable large employer' for purposes of the mandate requiring employers to provide health insurance
to mean an employer with at least 100 full-time employees
Document 10: repeal the 1993 increase in income taxes on Social Security benefits
effective January 1, 2007
